question: Which adjective means 'able to deal with the most difficult situations'?
She _____ constantly complaining about her workload. Her boss is getting frustrated.
Which phrase means 'to tolerate someone or something'?
Read: 'Luckily, he survived. When he was better, he was taken to Mr Brownlow, and was made to feel at home.' What does 'feel at home' mean?
choices: to feel comfortable in a particular place
to go back to your own house

question: We often use _____ to set the scene when describing past habits, and then use 'would' for other examples.
Which adjective describes someone who 'always tells people how great they are'?
Rewrite using a relative clause: 'I was talking about an app. It's really cool.'
choices: I was talking about an app which it's really cool.
I was talking about an app that is really cool.
I was talking about an app, which really cool.
I was talking about an app who is really cool.

question: Which phrase means 'to make someone feel foolish by laughing at them'?
Read: 'Many people today feel that relationships between members of the same family are not as close as they were a few generations ago.' This sentence expresses:
choices: what many people believe or feel

question: Which word is used for a 'fight or strong disagreement'?
2001 was the year _____ the terrorist attack happened.
Why did Oliver's brother Monks want Oliver to be a thief?
choices: because Monks was a generous person
because he wanted Oliver to be punished
so that Oliver would never be honest and Monks could keep all the money
because Oliver had stolen from Monks before

question: She _____ always make a fuss of him when they visited. (typical past behaviour - repeated action)
Which adjective describes a person who is 'not yet mentally or emotionally mature'?
Read: 'It is commonly accepted that the time needed to run a household has also decreased over the last few decades.' Which phrase signals an impersonal view?
What does 'went hungry' mean in the context of Oliver Twist?
The city _____ I was born has changed a lot.
Which phrase describes 'a newborn baby in the family'?
choices: a row with your best friend
a newborn baby in the family
a clash between you and a sibling

question: Khalil's life is completely dedicated to tackling pollution. He's _____ about it.
Which phrase in an opinion essay gives a PERSONAL opinion?
Rewrite using a participle clause: 'There are very few people who are still using analogue televisions.'
choices: There are very few people used analogue televisions.
There are very few people still using analogue televisions.
There are very few people to use analogue televisions.
There are very few people which using analogue televisions.

question: Which adjective describes someone who 'can always be trusted'?
Read: 'Changes in our working lives and the advent of technology have certainly altered how families interact.' What does 'altered' mean?
My brother _____ always shouting at me when we were kids. I found it very annoying.
Which word means 'to hate something or someone very much'?
What does 'a subsistence' refer to in the context of the workhouses?
The headset _____ came with my phone is really uncomfortable to wear.
Which phrase means 'to look at someone with admiration and respect'?
Read the essay extract: 'I would say that while society may have changed, families remain as close as they were.' This sentence is used in the _____ of the essay.
Which phrase means 'to disappoint someone'?
She _____ know all the answers when she was a student. (past state — no longer true)
Which phrase means 'to have someone you admire very highly, almost as if they are perfect'?
Jameela believes she's a better person than all her friends. She's so _____.
Read: 'Oliver decided to escape to London but had no food and went hungry for days, before he met another boy called Jack Dawkins.' Who showed Oliver the way to the city?
In a non-defining relative clause, the information given is:
Which phrase means 'to argue with your closest friend'?
choices: a newborn baby in the family
a row with your best friend

question: She was _____ arguing with her parents when she was younger. (annoying repeated habit in the past)
Which adjective describes someone who 'has high standards that are not always realistic'?
Read: 'Going online can be seen as a way of bringing families together.' The phrase 'can be seen as' is used to express:
Which phrase means 'to follow the example set by another person'?
